converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Gaston Liebert papers
Liébert, Gaston.
4435
3.2 cubic feet. 3.2 cubic feet.
1866-1929. Correspondence, clippings, letters, photographs. Letters, documents, and photographs of Gaston Liébert, including official correspondence with Paris and Hanoi and personal correspondence from other French diplomats in China and Indochina. Material emphasizes problems in the Sino-Vietnamese border area during 1899-1900 and activities of Vietnamese revolutionaries in China, Macao, and Hong Kong before and during World War I. There are also documents relating tariff and debt negotiations with China after the Boxer Rebellion and to an British commercial treaty with China. Collection material in English 2089975
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
B. Lockhart, M.E. Warren
Date completed:
January 1990
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, July 2003
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Gaston Ernest Liébert was born in Paris in 1866. He entered the Naval College in 1884 and served in the Navy for seven years. In 1893, he entered the diplomatic corps and was sent to the Far East in 1898. He served as French Vice-Consul to Pakhoi and Tunghing (China), 1899-1901; envoy for a special mission to the French Legation in Peking following the Boxer Rebellion, 1901-1902; French Consul to Hong Kong, 1903-1916; and Consul-General in New York, 1916
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Letters, documents, and photographs of Gaston Liébert, including official correspondence with Paris and Hanoi and personal correspondence from other French diplomats in China and Indochina. Material emphasizes problems in the Sino-Vietnamese border area during 1899-1900 and activities of Vietnamese revolutionaries in China, Macao, and Hong Kong before and during World War I. There are also documents relating tariff and debt negotiations with China after the Boxer Rebellion and to an British commercial treaty with China.
Included is a large mss. map, 1900; copies of dispatches and political directives; personal papers from Liébert's career, from Naval School in 1887, to his naval duty, and to his diplomatic and foreign affairs responsibilities in various places; social and family correspondence from his years in New York; an album of copies of documents and photographs relating to Liébert family history and genealogy; a 1901 correspondence book; and photographs and a diary from his tenure in Peking.
Cite As:
Gaston Liébert papers, #4435. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Consuls. Diplomats. China -- History -- Boxer Rebellion, 1899. China -- History -- Republic, 1912-1949. Vietnam -- History -- 1858-1945. Diplomatic and consular service, French. France -- Foreign relations -- China -- 20th century. Diplomatic and consular service -- United States. Diplomatic and consular service -- China. Nationalism -- Vietnam. Cường Đẻ̂. Lương, Ngọc Quyé̂n. Mai, Lão Bạng. Nguyễn, Thiên Thuật. Phan, Bội Châu, 1867-1940.
